svg.title: Searching Introduction

text.data-narration: Parsing is the process of analyzing a string of symbols, either in natural language or computer languages, conforming to the rules of a formal grammar. In compiler design, parsing is the second phase of compilation, following lexical analysis. The parser takes the stream of tokens produced by the lexical analyzer and verifies whether this stream can be generated by the grammar of the source language.

The primary goals of parsing are: 1. To determine if the input program is syntactically correct 2. To build a parse tree or abstract syntax tree that represents the syntactic structure of the program 3. To report syntax errors clearly and accurately.

circle.data-narration: Context-Free Grammars (CFG) are the foundation of parsing. A CFG consists of terminals, nonterminals, productions, and a start symbol.

text.data-narration: A CFG is used to define the syntax of programming languages. It consists of terminals, nonterminals, productions, and a start symbol.

circle.data-narration: Parse trees and abstract syntax trees are used to represent the syntactic structure of a program. They are hierarchical representations of the grammar rules used to generate the program.

text.data-narration: Parse trees and abstract syntax trees are used to represent the syntactic structure of a program. They are hierarchical representations of the grammar rules used to generate the program.

circle.data-narration: Semantic analyzers use the output of the parser to check the semantic correctness of the program. They check for errors such as type errors and scoping errors.

text.data-narration: Semantic analyzers use the output of the parser to check the semantic correctness of the program. They check for errors such as type errors and scoping errors.

circle.data-narration: Intermediate code generators use the output of the semantic analyzer to generate intermediate code. Intermediate code is machine code that is used for faster execution.

text.data-narration: Intermediate code generators use the output of the semantic analyzer to generate intermediate code. Intermediate code is machine code that is used for faster execution.

circle.data-narration: The compilation process consists of lexical analysis, parsing, semantic analysis, and intermediate code generation.

text.data-narration: The compilation process consists of lexical analysis, parsing, semantic analysis, and intermediate code generation.

circle.data-narration: The parser sits between the lexical analyzer and the semantic analyzer. It consumes tokens and produces a hierarchical structure that represents the grammatical relationships between those tokens.

text.data-narration: The parser sits between the lexical analyzer and the semantic analyzer. It consumes tokens and produces a hierarchical structure that represents the grammatical relationships between those tokens.

svg.text: Thank you for learning about Searching Introduction
